pages.raw_text:
Five years ago fully three-fourths
of the business property in Spokane
was owned outside of the city and
about a third of these holdings were
in the hands of foreigners.


In the five years just past a change
remarkable even in a section where
great changes are the rule has been
wrought, and today only about 10
per cent of the business houses pay
tribute in rentals to outside parties,
and very small fraction of this still
remains in foreign hands.


Taking the blocks fronting River
side ave. as fair instance, from
Monroe st. to Bernard, where the bus
mess congestion is greatest and
whic his the center of the city's life
a gratifying state of affairs in owner
ship exists, as follows:


Northside of Riverside ave, begin
ming at Monroe: Block 2S is owned
entirely in Spokane; block 20, adjoin-
ing, is owned by six parties, two of
whom are nonresident; block 10,
seven owners, all in Spokane; block
IA, five owners, one nonresident;
block Is, eight owners, one non-resi-
dent; block 16, four owners, one non
resident; block II, five owners, one
nonresident.


This is up to Bernard ST., and on
the south sid of Riverside, beginning
at Bernard: Block IS has six owners,
one nonresident; block 10, four own
ers, two nonresident; block 20, ten
owners, one nonresident; block 81,
seven owners, all of Spokane; block
%. the entire block is also owned
here; Llock 23, three of the II owners
are nonresident, and in block "a 10
of the ~ owners are residents of
Spokane.
